meta.source: Bluet official product brochures and technical PDFs
meta.pricingStatus: PLACEHOLDER - confirm real 'starting from' prices with Bluet at June 4 workshop
meta.note: Site depth minimums are for LLW (Lowest Low Water). Add local tidal/water level variation on top.

notes: Water depth below 2.0 m → site unsuitable for any Bluet product
Wave height above 0.3 m → breakwater required before installation
Land connection (electricity, water, sewage) is always mandatory
Floating foundations cannot be assembled when ice is present on site (Finnish installation window: ~May–November)

foundations.name: Bluet® Lightweight Foundation
foundations.notes: Plastic pontoon interiors can be used as tanks. Good for small sites and low structural loads.
foundations.recommendedFor: residential: NO, use Concrete instead. Terraces, walkways, saunas: YES.
foundations.variableFeesNote: Transport, installation, local supervision, localization, permits - quoted separately as % of product fee

foundations.name: Bluet® Modular Concrete Foundation

subtypes.name: Bluet® Concrete Megapontoon
subtypes.use: Large infrastructure: floating roads, bridges, parks, large area projects

keyAdvantages: Delivered with full MEP services (water, sewer, electricity)
High-quality EPS filling  - unsubmersible, no areas below water level
foundations.residentialNote: STRONGLY RECOMMENDED for residential. Steel is explicitly NOT recommended for housing (steel requires drydock maintenance every 5–10 years).
foundations.variableFeesNote: Transport, installation, local supervision, localization, permits - quoted separately as % of product fee

foundations.name: Bluet® Steel Foundation

siteConditions.notes: Can handle more extreme conditions than Lightweight or Concrete

keyAdvantages: Hollow barge spaces usable as technical rooms, storage, staff units
foundations.residentialNote: NOT recommended for residential. Requires drydock maintenance every 5–10 years.
foundations.basePriceRange: from €280,000 ex-works (indicative - large special projects only)
foundations.variableFeesNote: Transport, installation, local supervision, localization, permits - quoted separately as % of product fee

foundations.name: Bluet® Multiuse Platform

siteConditions.notes: NOT suitable for locations with open water fetch over 100 m or strong water flow/drag

technicalSpecs.maxRigidPlatformSize: ~20 m × 20 m (movement joint required above this)

foundations.assembly: 1–2 days on-site. Transportable by land (truck) or water (towing). No heavy lifting equipment needed.
foundations.variableFeesNote: Transport, installation, local supervision, localization, permits - quoted separately as % of product fee

pools.name: Bluet® Barge Pool
pools.description: Steel barge construction. Highest durability and strength. Ideal for large, prestigious projects. Withstands harsh conditions and ice.

siteRequirements.note: 3.0 m + water level variation (tidal/seasonal swing from normal to lowest low water)

dimensions.smallestConfiguration: 75 m² pool, 91 m² with deck (3 modules)

features: Infinity edge option
Underwater technical space inside barge
Space for pool cover (motorized remote-control cover)
Suitable for harsh wave and ice conditions
Stainless steel overflow gutters
pools.poolTechnology: Factory-made container installed under deck, inside barge, on-deck, or in separate technical space on land
pools.variableFeesNote: Transport, installation, local supervision, localization, permits - quoted separately as % of product fee

pools.name: Bluet® Multiuse Pool
pools.description: Versatile stainless-steel or coated modular pool. Works for children's wading pools up to trendy spa and nightclub pools. Assembled on water, no large land construction site needed. Can also be installed on land.

siteRequirements.note: 1.5 m + water level variation. Can also be installed on land.

features: Modular: scales from children's pool to large leisure pool
Assembled on water, no heavy lifting equipment
Land installation also possible
Wide accessory range: underwater benches, RGB lights, massage showers
Motorized pool cover included
pools.poolTechnology: Separate technical space needed on-deck or on land
pools.variableFeesNote: Transport, installation, local supervision, localization, permits - quoted separately as % of product fee

pools.name: Bluet® Hybrid Pool
pools.description: Cost-effective heated pool. Combines Grated and Barge pool designs. Polypropylene construction - rustproof, corrosion-free, acid-resistant. Suitable for both fresh and saltwater. Fits in a standard shipping container.

siteRequirements.note: Estimated - confirm with Bluet. Pool structure supported by surrounding pontoons.

dimensions.transportation: Optimized to fit in standard shipping container

pools.materials: Polypropylene cellular boards (walls and bottom) + acid-proof steel beam frame
features: Rustproof, corrosion-free, acid-resistant
Suitable for fresh AND saltwater
Can be assembled on water or on land
Cost-effective vs. Barge Pool
Insulated walls (note: less effective insulation than Barge Pool)
pools.poolTechnology: Pool technology module delivered in container, assembled on separate platform or on shore
pools.basePriceRange: from €95,000 ex-works (indicative - cost-effective vs. Barge Pool)
pools.variableFeesNote: Transport, installation, local supervision, localization, permits - quoted separately as % of product fee

pools.name: Bluet® Grated Pool
pools.description: Natural water pool with a rigid glass-fibre mesh bottom and sides. Safe swimming without worries about aquatic life or unpleasant seabed. Optional filtration system for poor water quality. Year-round use possible with de-icer pumps.
features: Glass-fibre reinforced plastic mesh grid bottom and sides
Year-round use with de-icer pumps in winter
Optional filtration system if natural water quality is poor
Can be built above water, no heavy lifting equipment required
Concrete pontoons surround the pool with deck cover
pools.waterQuality: Water quality analysis recommended. Optional filtration if surrounding water is poor.
pools.basePriceRange: from €70,000 ex-works (indicative - natural water, smaller structure)
pools.variableFeesNote: Transport, installation, local supervision, localization, permits - quoted separately as % of product fee

pools.name: Bluet® Bottomless Pool
pools.description: Open-bottom pool formed by surrounding platform modules. The simplest and most authentic natural water swimming experience. No filtering  - requires pure surrounding water.

siteRequirements.waterQuality: Surrounding water must be pure enough for swimming  - no filtering option available

features: Underwater safety nets always included
Wide selection of deck accessories
Can be located in sea, river, or lake
No filter system needed (or available)
pools.waterQualityWarning: NOT suitable if surrounding water quality is poor. Use Grated Pool with filtration instead.
pools.basePriceRange: from €40,000 ex-works (indicative  - simplest natural water option)
pools.variableFeesNote: Transport, installation, local supervision, localization, permits  - quoted separately as % of product fee

pools.name: Bluet® Heated Pool (General)
pools.description: When the customer wants a heated, filtered pool but hasn't chosen between Barge, Hybrid, or Multiuse variants. Default temperature 27°C, adjustable to thermal spa temperatures. Sizes up to Olympic 50 m.

poolTechnologyOptions.use: Standard  - lower investment, easy maintenance (sand change every 5–7 years)
Heavy commercial use  - most pure water quality, higher cost
Eco-premium  - no chemicals, natural water color, heats up to 32°C, lower operating costs

pools.note: Use Barge Pool for large/demanding sites, Hybrid Pool for cost-effective or saltwater sites, Multiuse Pool for shallow/flexible use.
pools.basePriceRange: from €85,000–195,000 ex-works depending on type (see specific pool products)
pools.variableFeesNote: Transport, installation, local supervision, localization, permits  - quoted separately as % of product fee

pools.name: Bluet® Customized Pool Solution
pools.description: Fully bespoke design for unique project needs. Not limited to standard modular structures. Can combine standard and tailored solutions.
pools.variableFeesNote: Transport, installation, local supervision, localization, permits  - quoted separately as % of product fee

anchoringOptions.name: Chain Anchoring
anchoringOptions.notes: Easier to install, more affordable. Common choice for most projects.

anchoringOptions.name: Pile Anchoring
anchoringOptions.notes: Higher initial cost. Sometimes mandatory depending on site conditions and local regulations.

anchoringOptions.name: Seaflex
anchoringOptions.bestFor: Specific site conditions where chain or pile is unsuitable
anchoringOptions.notes: Elastic mooring system - allows movement with water level variation.

anchoringOptions.name: Bluet® Mooring Arm
anchoringOptions.notes: Bluet's own product. Designed for sites where conventional anchoring is insufficient.

anchoringOptions.name: Spudleg System
anchoringOptions.notes: Only for Multiuse Platform. Temporary - does NOT increase load-bearing capacity. Requires water depth 1–5 m.

permitsFinland.note: Always flag these in the AI output. Permit lead times vary 6–18 months depending on municipality.

required.when: If site is not yet zoned for waterfront/floating construction
Always required for any floating structure affecting a waterway
For any structure with a superstructure (sauna, restaurant, housing etc.)
required.covers: Fire safety, access routes, accessibility, structural loads
Snow load, weight distribution, freeboard and stability, ice/water level extreme conditions
